nodes.title: Jamestown Settlement
nodes.description: The first permanent English settlement is established in Virginia, initiating colonial growth.

nodes.title: Mayflower Compact Signed
nodes.description: Pilgrim settlers sign the compact aboard the Mayflower, creating one of the earliest frameworks for self-governance in the New World.

nodes.title: Boston Tea Party
nodes.description: American colonists protest British taxation by dumping tea into Boston Harbor, fueling revolutionary sentiment.

nodes.title: Declaration of Independence
nodes.description: The Continental Congress declares independence from Great Britain, starting the Revolutionary War.

nodes.title: US Constitution Drafted
nodes.description: Delegates gather at Philadelphia to draft the Constitution, establishing the federal republic.

nodes.title: Louisiana Purchase
nodes.description: The United States purchases Louisiana Territory from France, doubling the national domain.

nodes.title: Manifest Destiny & Texas
nodes.description: Texas is annexed as the U.S. embraces the ideology of westward continental expansion.

nodes.title: California Gold Rush
nodes.description: Gold discovered at Sutter's Mill triggers a massive migration of over 300,000 people to California, accelerating western integration.

nodes.title: American Civil War
nodes.description: Conflict erupts between the Union and the seceded southern Confederacy over slavery and federal sovereignty.

nodes.title: Emancipation Proclamation
nodes.description: President Abraham Lincoln declares all slaves in confederate-held territory free, redefining the war's purpose.

nodes.title: Gilded Age Begins
nodes.description: An era of rapid economic and industrial expansion, coupled with immigration and urbanization.

nodes.title: Panama Canal Completed
nodes.description: The canal officially opens, connecting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans, drastically reducing shipping times and projecting global power.

nodes.title: U.S. Enters World War I
nodes.description: Abandoning neutrality, the United States joins the Allied Powers, swinging the conflict in their favor.

nodes.title: Women's Suffrage Ratified
nodes.description: The 19th Amendment is ratified, securing women the constitutionally guaranteed right to vote.

nodes.title: Great Depression
nodes.description: The Wall Street stock crash launches a decade of deep economic contraction and widespread poverty.

nodes.title: FDR's New Deal Programs
nodes.description: President Franklin D. Roosevelt launches massive government relief, recovery, and financial reform initiatives.

nodes.title: US Enters World War II
nodes.description: Following the attack on Pearl Harbor, the United States declares war and joins the Allies.

nodes.title: Truman Doctrine Declared
nodes.description: President Truman pledges support to democratic nations against authoritarian forces, formalizing the Cold War geopolitical structure.

nodes.title: Civil Rights Movement
nodes.description: The landmark Brown v. Board decision initiates a decades-long struggle for racial equality led by activists like Martin Luther King Jr.

nodes.title: Vietnam War Troop Deployment
nodes.description: The United States deploys combat troops to South Vietnam, escalating its military role in the containment of Communism.

nodes.title: Apollo Moon Landing
nodes.description: Apollo 11 astronaut Neil Armstrong becomes the first human to step on the lunar surface.

nodes.title: September 11 Attacks
nodes.description: Al-Qaeda terrorists hijack passenger planes and strike the World Trade Center and Pentagon, launching the War on Terror.

nodes.title: Digital & Mobile Boom
nodes.description: The release of the first iPhone accelerates the transition into the modern digital age, mobile internet, and global app networks.
